differential_diagnoses: Penetrating peptic ulcer of the duodenum with secondary retroperitoneal abscess
Perforated duodenal diverticulitis with secondary retroperitoneal abscess
Streptococcus anginosus bacteremia secondary to localized pyogenic infection
Antiphospholipid syndrome (primary or secondary to infection)
next_steps: Perform transthoracic or transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Perform urgent upper endoscopy (EGD) to evaluate the duodenal wall for ulceration or diverticula
Consult Interventional Radiology for aspiration or drainage of the suspected periduodenal/pararenal collection
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy based on S. anginosus susceptibility
Monitor coagulation status and evaluate for repeat testing of lupus anticoagulant after clinical recovery
Consult Infectious Disease for duration of therapy and management of potential deep-seated abscess
